message:
feat(analysis): add AnalysisConfig::triage_runtime() profile with tests and benchmarks

Add a lean analysis configuration that keeps only the graph metrics
consumed by triage, plan, and priority flows — PageRank, betweenness,
cycles, critical path, and articulation points — while skipping
eigenvector, HITS, k-core, and slack, which are only consumed by richer
insight surfaces.

graph.rs:
- Add AnalysisConfig::triage_runtime() const constructor.
- Add test verifying the config keeps articulation but skips insight-only
  metrics (eigenvector, HITS, k-core, slack).
- Add integration test proving compute_triage produces identical results
  under triage_runtime vs full config for a 4-issue dependency graph.

mod.rs:
- Add integration test that Analyzer::new_with_config(triage_runtime)
  produces identical triage, plan, and priority outputs as Analyzer::new()
  (full config) for the same issue set.

benches/triage.rs:
- Add analyzer_new_triage_runtime benchmark alongside the existing full
  analyzer benchmark in bench_real_fixture.
- Add bench_stress_fixture group exercising the 500-issue stress fixture
  with both full and triage_runtime configs.

The triage_runtime profile exists to reduce wall-clock latency for CLI
commands (--robot-triage, --robot-plan, --robot-priority, --emit-script,
etc.) that never inspect HITS/eigenvector/k-core/slack results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
